---
pagetitle: "Geospatial for Engineers"
description: >-
  Earth observation and geospatial data, explained for experienced software
  engineers with zero domain background.
---

```{=html}
<section class="hero" aria-labelledby="hero-thesis">
  <p class="eyebrow"><span class="comment">// </span>geospatial-for-engineers &middot; earth observation, without the domain gatekeeping</p>

  <div class="terminal">
    <div class="term-bar">
      <span class="term-dots" aria-hidden="true"><i></i><i></i><i></i></span>
      <span class="term-name">manifesto.txt &mdash; ~/geo &middot; <span class="coord">45.3&deg;N 12.5&deg;E</span></span>
      <span class="term-spacer"></span>
      <span class="term-crs" aria-hidden="true">WGS&nbsp;84</span>
    </div>
    <div class="term-body">
      <div class="hero-backdrop" aria-hidden="true">
        <svg class="hero-contours" viewBox="0 0 900 460" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" aria-hidden="true">
          <path class="sweep" d="M-20 120 C220 60 480 180 900 96"/>
          <path class="sweep" d="M-20 220 C240 168 520 288 900 214"/>
          <path class="sweep" d="M-20 330 C220 288 500 392 900 330"/>
          <path class="ring" d="M690 40 C810 78 856 190 792 288 C724 392 556 402 500 316 C452 244 500 128 610 88 C646 74 664 52 690 40Z"/>
          <path class="ring" d="M700 78 C796 110 832 200 780 280 C726 356 592 366 546 300 C508 246 546 150 634 118 C662 108 682 88 700 78Z"/>
          <path class="ring" d="M708 118 C782 144 810 204 770 268 C732 328 632 336 596 288 C568 250 596 182 662 156 C684 148 696 128 708 118Z"/>
          <path class="ring" d="M716 158 C770 180 790 220 762 262 C734 306 664 310 636 274 C616 246 636 200 682 180Z"/>
          <path class="ring" d="M722 198 C756 214 768 238 750 264 C732 292 692 292 674 268 C660 248 680 218 706 206Z"/>
        </svg>
      </div>
      <div class="hero-fore">
        <div class="hero-ticks" aria-hidden="true">GRID&nbsp;WGS&nbsp;84<br/><span class="val">EPSG:4326</span></div>
        <p class="cmd-line"><span class="prompt">~/geo $</span> <span class="cmd">cat manifesto.txt</span></p>
        <h1 class="hero-thesis" id="hero-thesis">Most geospatial tutorials explain the code and assume the domain. <span class="flip">This series does the opposite.</span><span class="cursor" aria-hidden="true"></span></h1>
        <p class="hero-tagline">
          Earth observation and geospatial data, explained for experienced
          software engineers with zero domain background.
        </p>
        <div class="hero-meta">
          <span class="tag"><span class="k">assumes:</span> JSON, CLI, data structures</span>
          <span class="tag teal"><span class="k">teaches:</span> CRS, coverage, raster, COG</span>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>
```

## Who this is for

You already write software. You are comfortable with JSON, the command line,
data structures, and reading an unfamiliar API. What you *don't* have is a
geospatial or earth-observation (EO) background: terms like `CRS`, `projection`,
`raster`, `coverage`, `footprint`, `COG`, and `Zarr` go by without quite landing,
and most tutorials assume the domain knowledge you're missing while carefully
explaining the code you already understand.

This series inverts that assumption. It spends its words on the **domain** (what
these concepts *mean* and *why they exist*) and trusts you with the code. It is
the on-ramp I wanted when I started doing EO work without the background.

```{=html}
<p class="gcards-label">The guides <span class="sub">&mdash; each self-contained; start wherever your problem is</span></p>
<a class="gcard" href="guides/coveragejson.html">
  <div class="gcard-head">
    <span class="dollar">$</span> open guides/coveragejson.qmd
    <span class="spacer"></span>
    <span class="coord">01 / 01</span>
  </div>
  <div class="gcard-body">
    <div>
      <h3 class="gcard-title">CoverageJSON Uncovered</h3>
      <p class="gcard-desc">
        A complete, ground-up guide to reading coverage data on the web, from
        your first document through the edge cases.
      </p>
    </div>
    <span class="gcard-cta">read the guide <span class="arrow">&rarr;</span></span>
  </div>
</a>
```
